title: 1. “I Must Destroy Him” | Gene and Roger
format: Episode 1 of The Ringer's 'Gene and Roger.' Brian Raftery traces how two rival Chicago newspaper critics became the country's most powerful thumbs, using the movies they fought over on air.

segments.heading: The Two Thumbs Era
segments.note: the films Gene and Roger fought over on air

films.title: Rocky IV
films.note: The cold open: Siskel defends it, Ebert begs Rocky to retire. America beats Russia; Paulie gets a robot.

films.title: RoboCop
films.note: The R-rated movie Raftery's parents wouldn't let him see, but Siskel & Ebert could air a clip of at 11 a.m.

films.title: My Dinner with Andre
films.note: Cited as proof that two thumbs up carried weight across the whole range of movies.

films.title: Anaconda
films.note: The other end of that range from My Dinner with Andre.

films.title: Reservoir Dogs
films.note: One of their stranger calls: both gave it a thumbs down.

films.title: Goodfellas
films.note: Ebert, almost dazed: 'a great American film.' Their reaction shows how big the movie felt in 1990.

films.title: Die Hard
films.note: Ebert thumbs down over the plot holes; Siskel stayed interested.

films.title: The Silence of the Lambs
films.note: Siskel voted thumbs down; Ebert didn't buy it either. 'This is the movies.'

segments.heading: Before Siskel and Ebert
segments.note: the critics and films that set the table

films.title: The Shining
films.note: Gene Shalit's tightly scripted pun on the Today Show: 'Kubrick is responsible for the dulling of The Shining.'

films.title: Conan the Barbarian
films.note: Tarantino's example of the local-newscast critic as consumer advocate: should you waste your money?

films.title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
films.note: Among the films Siskel and Ebert covered in their disastrous, barely-seen 1975 pilot.

films.title: Dog Day Afternoon
films.note: Also in the pilot, back when film, not TV, led the cultural conversation.

segments.heading: This Season on Gene and Roger

films.title: Pulp Fiction
films.note: Cannes teaser: Ebert saw it and couldn't decide if it was one of the best movies he'd ever seen or one of the worst.

Ads: Netflix FIFA World Cup Launch Edition

One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest.title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
